name: Multi-Page Form Layout (Design System Basic Layout Pattern)
summary: Categorization with `variant: pages` implements the Design System's basic layout pattern — the preferred method for complex government forms. Creates a Task List (table of contents) with progress tracking, section groupings, and a summary review page.
whenToUse: Complex forms with 6+ fields or multiple logical sections
When the form benefits from breaking content into digestible steps
Government service applications that collect personal info, contact info, and supporting details
When you want the Design System's recommended Task List pattern with progress tracking
When users need to see application status at a glance and enter at any step

categorizationOptions.description: All available options on the Categorization element when variant is 'pages'

options.name: variant
options.description: Set to 'pages' for the Design System's page stepper with Task List, or 'stepper' for a simple stepper with navigation tabs

options.name: title
options.description: Title displayed at the top of the Task List page

options.name: subtitle
options.description: Subtitle displayed below the title on the Task List page

options.name: hideSummary
options.description: When true, hides the Summary row on the Task List and skips the summary review page entirely

options.name: hideSubmit
options.description: When true, hides the Submit button on the summary review page (the review page still renders)

options.name: toAppOverviewLabel
options.description: Custom text for the back link that returns the user from a step page to the Task List

options.name: hideProgress
options.description: When true, hides the progress/completion indicators on the Task List

options.name: additionalInstructions
options.description: Instructions displayed on the Task List page. Can be a plain string or an object with { content: string } for markdown content

categoryOptions.description: All available options on Category elements within a pages-variant Categorization

options.name: sectionTitle
options.description: Groups one or more categories under a section heading on the Task List. Categories sharing the same sectionTitle are grouped together. Sections only appear on the Task List for organizational purposes.

options.name: showInTaskList
options.description: When false, hides the category from the Task List. The category page still renders but does not get its own row, does not show a section header by itself, and is excluded from the completed-page count. If a hidden category follows a visible task in the same section, its completion is rolled into the visible task.

notes: Use Categorization with `variant: "pages"` as the root UI schema element — this is the Design System's preferred method for complex forms
Each Category becomes a separate page/step in the form
Give each Category a descriptive `label` — this is shown as the page title and Task List row
Group related fields into the same Category
Use `sectionTitle` on Category options to group multiple tasks under a section heading on the Task List
Categories sharing the same `sectionTitle` value are grouped under one section heading
A Summary review page is automatically appended for applicant review before submission
Set `hideSummary: true` if the form doesn't need a review step (e.g. simple data collection)
Set `hideSubmit: true` if submission is handled elsewhere (e.g. by a parent application)
Use `toAppOverviewLabel` to customize the back link text (e.g. 'Back to task list')
Set `hideProgress: true` to hide completion indicators on the Task List
Use `additionalInstructions` to display guidance text on the Task List page
Set `showInTaskList: false` on a Category to hide it from the Task List — useful for sub-steps that should be part of a parent task's flow

CRITICAL: Category elements can ONLY be direct first-level children of the Categorization `elements` array. Do NOT wrap Categories in VerticalLayout, Group, or any other layout — only Category is valid at the first level inside a Categorization
